input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
dotnet workload install maui
# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows target needs the Windows App SDK and WinUI components. The easiest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want the build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# From an elevated prompt — adjust path if using BuildTools
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --passive --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set up the Android SDK

```powershell
# Install OpenJDK (MAUI needs JDK 17)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Install Android command-line tools
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
mkdir -Force $androidHome

# Download the latest command-line tools
Invoke-WebRequest `
  -Uri "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip" `
  -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp"

# The tools expect a specific directory layout
mkdir -Force "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest"
Move-Item "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp\cmdline-tools\*" `
  "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\" -Force
Remove-Item "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp" -Recurse
```

## Step 5 — Set environment variables (elevated prompt)

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidHome, "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17", "User")

# Add tools to PATH
$path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
$additions = @(
  "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin",
  "$androidHome\platform-tools"
) -join ";"
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "$path;$additions", "User")

# Reload in current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidHome
$env:JAVA_HOME = "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17"
$env:Path += ";$additions"
```

## Step 6 — Discover and install the right Android SDK packages

```powershell
# List available platforms & build-tools dynamically
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select -First 10
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;" | Select -First 10

# Install what MAUI needs (adjust versions from the list above)
sdkmanager --install `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  "emulator"

# Accept all licenses
sdkmanager --licenses   # answer 'y' to all
```

## Step 7 — Create an Android emulator (optional)

```powershell
avdmanager create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" `
  -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  -d "pixel_7"

# Launch it
emulator -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## Step 8 — Enable Windows Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Elevated prompt
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## Step 9 — Create, build, and run a test project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (emulator must be running)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## Step 10 — Verify everything

```powershell
dotnet workload list           # should show "maui"
sdkmanager --list_installed    # should show platforms, build-tools, etc.
java --version                 # should show 17.x
dotnet --info                  # check SDK version & runtimes
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Adjust `android-35` / `build-tools;35.0.0` to whatever `sdkmanager --list` shows as latest — Step 6 discovers this dynamically.
- The `net9.0-*` TFMs assume .NET 9; substitute `net8.0-*` if using .NET 8.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for emulator hardware acceleration (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
